name: Saba of the Long Road
role: Dying Wend elder trying to reach a place she left as a child
voiceHints: Warm, unhurried, tells the long version because the long version is the point. Carries every road she's walked in how she speaks. Dying and unafraid of it, only afraid of not finishing.
knowledge: Every road, ford, and danger of the Unspooling and its marches
The Wend's ways of reading a stranger as kin or not
Where she's going, and why it matters more than her life
wants: To reach the place of her earliest memory before she dies, and to know the Wend will carry on without her.
fears: Dying on the road with the last stretch unwalked, and leaving the road-family unheld.

reactsToReputation.kind: adopts them into the Wend on the spot
reactsToReputation.reliable: trusts them to guide the family after
reactsToReputation.callous: reads them true, and grieves the reading

appearance: Very old and visibly dying — skin gone papery over the bones of the face, white hair thin enough to show the scalp, a wanderer's frame reduced to almost nothing. Road-dust in every crease. The eyes are still fiercely alive.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.
